methods.method_name: X-ray Diffraction Analysis of SiO2 Lithiation
methods.description: This method involves analyzing the progression of SiO2 lithiation using X-ray diffraction (XRD) to demonstrate the irreversible conversion of crystalline SiO2 to other phases. The method aims to enhance the SiO2 signal by preparing new electrodes with a specific silica to carbon black to binder ratio.

steps.description: Prepare new electrodes with a silica : carbon black : binder ratio of 75 : 15 : 10.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare electrodes with specific material ratio

steps.description: Analyze the XRD pattern of the pristine electrode to identify the main peak of cristobalite and other features.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze XRD pattern of pristine electrode

steps.description: Analyze the XRD pattern of an electrode subjected to one lithiation cycle and 48 h potentiostatic hold step.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze XRD pattern after lithiation cycle

steps.description: Analyze the XRD pattern of an electrode subjected to the entire electrochemical activation pretreatment (5 full cycles) to observe the disappearance of cristobalite reflections.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze XRD pattern after full electrochemical activation
